title: Stop-hook ask gate: three-stops filter + guard-fired marker lifecycle (FEAT-013 pillar 3 — the chaos-driver foreclosure)
description: A Stop hook (the only mechanical point that sees a turn's final message; currently unused) that blocks turn-end on an ask failing the three-legitimate-stops filter. Three stops PASS: a pending provenance grant (guard-fired marker set); an escalation naming why the choice is underdetermined (decision_escalates_underdetermined shape); a HELD action awaiting authorization. Every other ask-shaped closer ("on your go", "now or later", "shall I", "want me to") is relitigation — blocked, governing rule named. Regex first; invokeMonitor classification if regex is too blunt. Also clears the guard-fired marker at a genuine turn boundary (closing the same-turn sentinel self-attestation hole with the guard). FEAT-013 R6, R12.
acceptance_criteria: A turn ending on an ask that fails the three-stops filter is blocked with stderr naming the governing rule; the work proceeds instead.
Each of the three legitimate stops passes the gate (a pending provenance table, an underdetermined-escalation ask, a HELD-action authorization ask).
The Stop hook clears the TASK-096 guard-fired marker at turn boundary; a same-turn sentinel-bearing re-issue on any sentinel surface is blocked (lifecycle per TASK-096).
Regression cells: relitigation-ask blocked, each legitimate stop passed, marker-lifecycle across the sentinel surfaces.
